2605.27348.title: When Eyes Betray AI: Social Gaze Consistency as a Semantic Cue for AI-Generated Image Detection
2605.27348.paper_summary: Recent generative models have largely closed the gap on low-level artifacts - pixel fingerprints, frequency anomalies, upsampling traces - particularly in person-centric and partial-edit settings where the manipulated region is small and surrounded by photometrically authentic content. We introduce Social Gaze Consistency, a high-level semantic cue defined as the mutual coherence of gaze direction, head-eye alignment, and pupil placement between interacting individuals, and show that it constitutes a previously underutilized detection axis orthogonal to existing low-level paradigms. We instantiate this insight through three coupled mechanisms: (i) a controlled diagnostic dataset with region-specific perturbations of gaze-consistent imagery, where strict pair-level grouping forecloses generator-fingerprint memorization as an optimization-time shortcut rather than relying on augmentation; (ii) Block-Compositional Caption Supervision, which holds a single 5-block reasoning skeleton invariant across 1,250 macro-combined captions, decoupling reasoning consistency from surface diversity; (iii) Cross-architecture validation showing the same supervision improves a vision-language backbone (FakeVLM) by +3.7 pp on the COCOAI Interaction subset (balanced accuracy 67.8 -> 71.5) and +1.3 pp on the COCOAI Person subset (83.0 -> 84.3), with consistent gains on a vision-only backbone (Effort), evidencing a backbone-agnostic cue. Real- and fake-class recalls rise simultaneously, ruling out a "predict-all-fake" artifact. A four-step mechanistic account - paired-edit shortcut blocking, hard-to-easy difficulty transfer, CLIP prior preservation, and diffusion-family shared spectral weakness in periocular structure - explains why training on a single inpainter (FLUX.1-Fill) transfers to multi-generator suites. We will release the code upon acceptance to facilitate reproducibility.
2605.27348.paper_summary_zh: 最近的生成模型在低階人工痕跡方面已大幅縮小了差距——像素指紋、頻率異常、上採樣痕跡——特別是在以人為中心和部分編輯的情境中，當被操控的區域小且被光度上真實的內容包圍時。我們引入了社交凝視一致性，這是一種高階語義線索，定義為互動個體之間凝視方向、頭眼對齊和瞳孔位置的相互一致性，並顯示它構成了一個先前未被充分利用的檢測軸，與現有的低階範式正交。我們通過三個耦合機制來具現化這一見解：(i) 一個受控的診斷數據集，具有凝視一致影像的區域特定擾動，其中嚴格的配對級分組阻止了生成器指紋的記憶化，作為優化時間的捷徑，而不是依賴增強；(ii) 區塊組合標題監督，保持單一的5區塊推理骨架在1,250個宏觀組合標題中不變，將推理一致性與表面多樣性解耦；(iii) 跨架構驗證顯示相同的監督提高了一個視覺-語言主幹（FakeVLM）在COCOAI互動子集上的表現+3.7個百分點（平衡準確率67.8 -> 71.5）和在COCOAI人子集上的表現+1.3個百分點（83.0 -> 84.3），在僅視覺主幹（Effort）上也有一致的增益，證明這是一個主幹無關的線索。真實和假類別的召回率同時上升，排除了“預測所有假”的人工痕跡。一個四步機械解釋——配對編輯捷徑阻塞、難度從難到易轉移、CLIP先驗保留，以及在眼周結構中的擴散家族共享光譜弱點——解釋了為什麼在單一填補器（FLUX.1-Fill）上的訓練可以轉移到多生成器套件。我們將在接受後釋出代碼以促進可重複性。
2605.27348.author: Jihyeon Kim et.al.
2605.27348.authors: Jihyeon Kim, Sohee Kim, Soosan Lee, Souhwan Jung, James Matthew Rehg, Hyesong Choi
